root_text.an4.24:1.1: Ekaṁ samayaṁ bhagavā sākete viharati kāḷakārāme.
root_text.an4.24:2.1: Yaṁ, bhikkhave, sadevakassa lokassa samārakassa sabrahmakassa sassamaṇabrāhmaṇiyā pajāya sadevamanussāya diṭṭhaṁ sutaṁ mutaṁ viññātaṁ pattaṁ pariyesitaṁ anuvicaritaṁ manasā, tamahaṁ jānāmi.
root_text.an4.24:3.1: Yaṁ, bhikkhave, sadevakassa lokassa samārakassa sabrahmakassa sassamaṇabrāhmaṇiyā pajāya sadevamanussāya diṭṭhaṁ sutaṁ mutaṁ viññātaṁ pattaṁ pariyesitaṁ anuvicaritaṁ manasā, tamahaṁ abbhaññāsiṁ.
root_text.an4.24:3.2: Taṁ tathāgatassa viditaṁ, taṁ tathāgato na upaṭṭhāsi.
root_text.an4.24:4.1: Yaṁ, bhikkhave, sadevakassa lokassa samārakassa sabrahmakassa sassamaṇabrāhmaṇiyā pajāya sadevamanussāya diṭṭhaṁ sutaṁ mutaṁ viññātaṁ pattaṁ pariyesitaṁ anuvicaritaṁ manasā, tamahaṁ na jānāmīti vadeyyaṁ, taṁ mamassa musā.
root_text.an4.24:5.1: Yaṁ, bhikkhave …pe… tamahaṁ jānāmi ca na ca jānāmīti vadeyyaṁ, tampassa tādisameva.
root_text.an4.24:6.1: Yaṁ, bhikkhave …pe… tamahaṁ neva jānāmi na na jānāmīti vadeyyaṁ, taṁ mamassa kali.
root_text.an4.24:7.1: Iti kho, bhikkhave, tathāgato daṭṭhā daṭṭhabbaṁ, diṭṭhaṁ na maññati, adiṭṭhaṁ na maññati, daṭṭhabbaṁ na maññati, daṭṭhāraṁ na maññati;
root_text.an4.24:7.2: sutvā sotabbaṁ, sutaṁ na maññati, asutaṁ na maññati, sotabbaṁ na maññati, sotāraṁ na maññati;
root_text.an4.24:7.3: mutvā motabbaṁ, mutaṁ na maññati, amutaṁ na maññati, motabbaṁ na maññati, motāraṁ na maññati;
root_text.an4.24:7.4: viññatvā viññātabbaṁ, viññātaṁ na maññati, aviññātaṁ na maññati, viññātabbaṁ na maññati, viññātāraṁ na maññati.
root_text.an4.24:7.5: Iti kho, bhikkhave, tathāgato diṭṭhasutamutaviññātabbesu dhammesu tādīyeva tādī.
root_text.an4.24:7.6: ‘Tamhā ca pana tādimhā añño tādī uttaritaro vā paṇītataro vā natthī’ti vadāmīti.
root_text.an4.24:8.1: Yaṁ kiñci diṭṭhaṁva sutaṁ mutaṁ vā,

translation_text.an4.24:1.1: At one time the Buddha was staying near Sāketa, in Kāḷaka’s monastery.
translation_text.an4.24:1.2: There the Buddha addressed the mendicants,
translation_text.an4.24:2.1: “In this world—with its gods, Māras, and divinities, this population with its ascetics and brahmins, its gods and humans—whatever is seen, heard, thought, known, attained, sought, and explored by the mind: that I know.
translation_text.an4.24:3.1: In this world—with its gods, Māras, and Divinities, this population with its ascetics and brahmins, its gods and humans—whatever is seen, heard, thought, known, attained, sought, and explored by the mind: that I have insight into.
translation_text.an4.24:3.2: That has been known by a Realized One, but a Realized One is not subject to it.
translation_text.an4.24:4.1: If I were to say that ‘I do not know … the world with its gods’, I would be lying.
translation_text.an4.24:5.1: If I were to say that ‘I both know and do not know … the world with its gods’, that would be just the same.
translation_text.an4.24:6.1: If I were to say that ‘I neither know nor do not know … the world with its gods’, that would be my fault.
translation_text.an4.24:7.1: So a Realized One sees what is to be seen, but does not conceive what is seen, does not conceive what is unseen, does not conceive what is to be seen, and does not conceive a seer.
translation_text.an4.24:7.2: He hears what is to be heard, but does not conceive what is heard, does not conceive what is unheard, does not conceive what is to be heard, and does not conceive a hearer.
translation_text.an4.24:7.3: He thinks what is to be thought, but does not conceive what is thought, does not conceive what is not thought, does not conceive what is to be thought, and does not conceive a thinker.
translation_text.an4.24:7.4: He knows what is to be known, but does not conceive what is known, does not conceive what is unknown, does not conceive what is to be known, and does not conceive a knower.
translation_text.an4.24:7.5: Since a Realized One is unaffected in the midst of things that ought be seen, heard, thought, and known, he is the unaffected one.
translation_text.an4.24:7.6: And I say that there is no better or finer poise than this.
translation_text.an4.24:8.1: Such a one does not take anything
translation_text.an4.24:8.2: seen, heard, or thought to be ultimately true or false.
translation_text.an4.24:8.3: But others get attached, thinking it’s the truth,
translation_text.an4.24:9.2: to which people are attached and cling,
translation_text.an4.24:9.3: saying, ‘I know, I see, that’s how it is’,
translation_text.an4.24:9.4: the Realized Ones have no attachments.”

variant_text.an4.24:5.1: tampassa → taṁ pissa (sya-all, km); taṁ mamassa (mr)
variant_text.an4.24:7.5: tādīyeva tādī → tādisova tādī (sya-all, km); tādise yeva tādi (pts1ed); tādīyeva tādīyevekā (mr)
